purpose: Technical implementation plan to create Global HR Dataverse tables in dependency order, configure relationships, security, and CSV/API load sequencing.

summary.recommended_build_pattern: Create tables and alternate keys first, then lookups/relationships, then security profiles, then data loads by dependency order.

environment_prerequisites: Create or select Power Platform environment with Dataverse enabled.
Create unmanaged solution for development and future managed export.
Confirm service account permissions for Dataverse table creation and data import.
Store CSV files in controlled SharePoint or Azure storage path before automated load.

security.recommended_dataverse_access_model: team-based security role + business unit filter where applicable

data_load_plan.global_pattern: Upsert by alternate key using CSV source columns; load parent/reference tables before child/transaction tables.

pre_build_checks: Confirm every source table has a Dataverse entity logical name.
Confirm every primary key is unique and non-null.
Confirm every relationship target table exists in table_build_plan.
Confirm restricted fields are covered by field security profile design.
post_build_checks: All 76 relationships created or explicitly deferred.
All security teams and roles assigned.
Row count reconciliation matches source JSON/CSV catalog.
Lookup resolution error rate equals zero or is documented.
known_considerations: Self-referencing relationships should be created after base table exists.
Large tables should use batching and retry policy.
Restricted Compensation, Payroll, Talent and Audit fields require field-level security review.
Some source columns named *_id are kept as text and also used to create lookups; implementation may choose to retain both external id and Dataverse lookup column.
Relationships REL073-REL076 reference dataset_tables as logical governance metadata, not one of the 41 physical CSV entities. Create dataset_tables only if a governance catalog table is desired.
